message.content:
Here’s a template you can adapt. I’ll keep it professional, clear, and tactfully honest without drifting into bitterness.

---

[Your Name]  
[Your Address]  
[City, State ZIP]  
[Email Address]  
[Phone Number]  

[Date]

[Manager’s Name]  
[Their Title]  
[Company Name]  
[Company Address]  
[City, State ZIP]

Dear [Manager’s Name],

I am writing to formally resign from my position as [Your Title] at [Company], effective [last working day, typically two weeks from date].

This was not a decision I made lightly. Over time, I’ve realized that the way the role is structured and the overall working environment are not a good fit for how I work best or for my long-term goals. I’ve given this considerable thought, and I believe it is in both my interest and the company’s interest that I move on.

I appreciate the opportunities I’ve had to develop my skills in [briefly mention one or two areas, even if you had to fight for them: e.g., “client communication,” “project coordination,” “technical work on X”]. Those experiences have clarified the kind of work culture and expectations in which I can contribute most effectively, even if this particular setting hasn’t been the right match.

During my notice period, I am committed to making the transition as smooth as possible. I’m happy to document my responsibilities, hand off ongoing projects, and assist in training a replacement or colleague to take over my duties.

Thank you for the opportunity to be part of [Company]. I wish you and the team the best moving forward.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

---

If you want it a bit more candid without crossing the line, you can adjust some key sentences. For example:

- “the way the role is structured and the overall working environment are not a good fit” could be changed to  
  “the expectations of the role and the working environment are not aligned with how I can do my best work.”

- If you want to nod to specific issues without sounding accusatory:  
  “This experience has given me clarity about the kind of communication, support, and priorities I need to be effective in a role.”

Paste your draft here if you’d like line‑by‑line tweaks for tone.
